formats.name: Signs of...
formats.description: List-based format identifying overlooked signs of a mental state. Highly relatable; readers self-diagnose silently while swiping.
formats.slide_pattern: Each middle slide = one sign + a 1-sentence elaboration showing how it actually feels in daily life.
examples: 7 Signs You're Burnt Out, Not Lazy
6 Signs You're Carrying More Than You Realize
5 Signs of High-Functioning Anxiety in Men

formats.name: Reframe
formats.description: Replaces unhealthy self-talk or beliefs with healthier alternatives. High save rate.
formats.slide_pattern: Each middle slide = one old-thought / new-thought pair with a brief why.
examples: Stop Saying 'I'm Fine'. Try These 5 Honest Answers.
5 Things to Say Instead of 'Man Up'
Reframe These 6 Thoughts and Sleep Better Tonight

formats.name: Myth-busting
formats.description: Challenges common misconceptions. Drives shares and comments because it provokes disagreement.
formats.slide_pattern: Each middle slide = the myth + the truth + 1 sentence of context.
examples: 5 Mental Health Myths Holding Men Back
6 Lies You've Been Told About Asking for Help
4 Myths About Therapy Men Need to Stop Believing

formats.name: How-to / Process
formats.description: Step-by-step guide. Highly saved as reference, strong completion rate.
formats.slide_pattern: Each middle slide = one numbered step + practical action a reader can take today.
examples: How to Talk to Your Partner About Your Anxiety in 6 Steps
How to Break the 3am Anxiety Loop in 5 Steps
How to Start Therapy When You Hate the Idea: 7 Steps

formats.name: Truth-bomb / Vulnerability
formats.hook_template: Things Men [AGE+] Won't Say Out Loud
formats.description: Direct, vulnerable truths that men recognize but don't articulate. Highest male-vulnerability engagement per CORQ 2026 data.
formats.slide_pattern: Each middle slide = one short truth statement + a single sentence of permission/validation.
examples: 7 Things Men Over 35 Don't Say Out Loud
6 Truths About Loneliness Men Are Too Proud to Admit
5 Things You're Allowed to Feel as a Man

formats.name: Comparison / vs.
formats.description: Side-by-side educational comparison. High save rate as reference content.
formats.slide_pattern: Each middle slide = one criterion with how A vs. B differ on it.
examples: Burnout vs. Depression: 5 Differences That Matter
Stress vs. Anxiety: How to Tell Them Apart
